figures.caption: TABLE I. The G-type Antiferromagnetic structure of Rb1−δV2Te2O is described under its magnetic space group (MagSG, crystallographic description), with basic information about its relation to its parent paramagnetic structure. All items in the table are supported by the magnetic CIF format (mcif).

FIG. 3. (Color online) The results of Rietveld refinements of the NPD data obtained from TREND at (a) T = 350 K, and (b)(c) T = 5 K, respectively. Panel (b) and (c) show the 5 K data fitted with the magnetic phase modeled using two different methodologies, respectively, which result in the same G-type AFM structure. The asterisks denote the peaks from a minor impurity phase, while the black arrows in panels (b) and (c) indicate the position of two strong magnetic peaks.
FIG. 1. (Color online) The illustration of two typical magnetic structures of Rb1−δV2Te2O. The G-type and C-type AFM structures are displayed in panel (a) and (b), respectively.

FIG. 2. (Color online) Magnetic susceptibility χ versus T of Rb1−δV2Te2O polycrystal under a magnetic field of 1 T, where the positions of two transitions TN and T ∗ are noted by the blue and purple arrows, respectively. The drop at around 150 K are related to the structural transition of V2O3.
